results.possibleIdentity: Marc Shapiro was an electronics engineer active during the late 1960s and early 1970s, based in New York. He worked for Astrosystems Incorporated, a company that operated in New Hyde Park (Lake Success) and New Rochelle, New York, specializing in the manufacture of precision digital instrumentation, automatic test equipment, and synchro-to-digital converters. Shapiro's technical expertise was notably diverse, including audio engineering, medical electronics, digital equipment, and the development of psychological testing machines. He was an engineer-member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established to foster collaborations between the scientific/engineering community and artists. His professional profile and technical specialties were recorded in the E.A.T. Technical Services directory, intended to match artists with engineers capable of providing specialized technical assistance for complex art installations and technological projects.
